title: Factors affecting the sources of finance
summary: 9609 AS — purpose, amount, duration, control, cost, and availability factors in finance decisions.
learningObjectives: Explain impact of amount and duration required.
Apply factors to case study decisions.

simpleExplanation.title: Which source fits?
simpleExplanation.summary: The right source depends on why money is needed, how much, for how long, whether owners will share control, and the cost of capital.
steps: Purpose: fixed asset vs working capital vs start-up.
Amount: overdraft for small gaps vs share issue for millions.
Control: debt avoids dilution; equity shares control.
Cost and availability: interest rates, credit rating, market conditions.

flashcards.back: Fixed assets → loan/leasing; working capital → overdraft/trade credit; start-up → equity/VC.
Small sums → overdraft or trade credit; large → share issue or long-term loan.
Match repayment to asset life — don't use overdraft for 10-year factory.
Owners wanting control prefer debt; equity brings new shareholders and possible dilution.
Interest on debt; dividends/opportunity cost on equity; arrangement fees.
More debt raises gearing and interest risk — link to 10.2.4 at A Level.
Credit rating, collateral, economic conditions affect whether banks will lend.
Start-ups lack retained profit and track record; PLCs can access capital markets.

sections.content: **Factors affecting finance** (5.2.3) feeds directly into **5.2.4 selection**. In **analyse** questions, take each factor from the syllabus and **apply it to the case** — generic lists score fewer marks.

items: **Purpose** — revenue vs capital expenditure; start-up vs expansion.
**Control** — family firm may reject external equity.
**Risk/gearing** — ability to service debt from cash flow.

sections.question: A family-owned Ltd needs $2 m for a new factory. Owners refuse to lose control. Interest rates are high. Discuss two relevant factors.
sections.solution:
**Control:** Owners reject **share issue** to outsiders — prefer **debt** (bank loan, debentures) to keep 100% ownership, accepting higher **gearing** and interest cost.

**Cost of finance:** **High interest rates** make debt expensive — may delay project, seek **government grant**, or use **retained profit** partially to reduce borrowing.

Also consider **duration** — 15-year factory matched to **long-term loan**, not overdraft.
